content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers and QQQ queries. Each query is described by two integers LLL and RRR. For each query, output the number of tuples (i,j,k)(i, j, k)(i,j,k) such that L‚â§i<j<k‚â§RL\le i\lt j\lt k\le RL‚â§i<j<k‚â§R and Ai+Aj+AkA_i+A_j+A_kAi‚Äã+Aj‚Äã+Ak‚Äã is an even number.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and QQQ.
The next line contains NNN integers A1,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
Then QQQ lines follow, each containing two integers LiL_iLi‚Äã and RiR_iRi‚Äã.


Output Format
For each query, output the number of tuples possible as mentioned in the problem statement.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query, we can choose (1,2,3)(1, 2, 3)(1,2,3) since A1+A2+A3=6A_1 + A_2 + A_3 = 6A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã+A3‚Äã=6 is an even
number.
For the second query, we can choose (2,3,5)(2, 3, 5)(2,3,5) since A2+A3+A5=10A_2 + A_3 + A_5 = 10A2‚Äã+A3‚Äã+A5‚Äã=10 is even, and (3,4,5)(3, 4, 5)(3,4,5) since A3+A4+A5=12A_3 + A_4 + A_5 = 12A3‚Äã+A4‚Äã+A5‚Äã=12 is even.
